records.ety: [L. expiatus, p. p. of expiare to expiate; ex out + piare to seek to appease, to purify with sacred rites, fr. pius pious. See Pious.]
records.morph: [imp. & p. p. Expiated(?); p. pr. & vb. n. Expiating(?).]

defs: To extinguish the guilt of by sufferance of penalty or some equivalent; to make complete satisfaction for; to atone for; to make amends for; to make expiation for; as, to expiate a crime, a guilt, or sin.
quotes: To expiate his treason, hath naught left.
The Treasurer obliged himself to expiate the injury.
Neither let there be found among you any one that shall expiate his son or daughter, making them to pass through the fire.
